_provenance.writtenBy: stardust:adopt (manual stage-2 prep — NOT by stardust:extract)
synthesizedInputs: site.tagline (DESIGN.md description line — Adobe Acrobat Studio is the design reference, not a deployed site)
voiceSamples.toneMetrics (counted from DESIGN.json voice + voiceTable + the two source HTMLs)
register field (declared brand — marketing surface DS, not product UI)
_provenance.notes: Mirrors the shape of stardust/current/_brand-extraction.json so source/target can be read side-by-side when authoring PRODUCT.md and downstream prototypes. Authored from already-distilled DESIGN artifacts plus verbatim copy in the two reference HTMLs. Voice samples are direct quotes from designs/hub/index.html and designs/bizpro-hub-prototype (1)/bizpro-hub.html — no paraphrase. Several source-side fields (e.g. crossPromo, voiceTable.headingFrequency over 5 crawled pages) have no clean target counterpart because the target is a 2-file design reference, not a crawled site — those are marked _provenance: not-applicable.

site.name: Acrobat Studio
site.tagline: Confident, motion-led marketing surface — Adobe Clean Display Black, restrained light palette, signature blue trial CTA, scroll-driven mosaic and text reveals.
site.originUrl: designs/hub/ + designs/bizpro-hub-prototype (1)/ (local design references; no deployed origin)
site._provenance: DESIGN.md frontmatter line 32 (description) + extensions.site in DESIGN.json

logo.notes: Adobe wordmark. The gnav crossfades the white variant over the hero into the black variant on scroll (display:none swap, not opacity blend). The red wordmark is favicon-only — Adobe Red (#eb1000) is reserved per The Adobe-Red Reservation rule.

palette._source: DESIGN.md colors.surface-light; mega-nav panel + perk-card + plan-card grounds
DESIGN.md colors.surface-soft; editorial bento + app-card + hub-router-card grounds
DESIGN.md colors.text-muted; eyebrow on perk + section headers
DESIGN.md colors.trial-blue; reserved per The One Blue Rule — only on Start free trial CTA + plan-trial pill
DESIGN.md colors.adobe-red; wordmark + favicon only — never a UI accent

headingFamily.name: Adobe Clean Display
headingFamily.stack: 'Adobe Clean Display', system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif

bodyFamily.name: Adobe Clean
bodyFamily.stack: 'Adobe Clean', 'Adobe Clean Display', system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif

scaleAudit._provenance: Acrobat scale: 96 → 80 → 56 → 48 → 24. Ratios are 96/80=1.20, 80/56=1.43, 56/48=1.17, 48/24=2.0 — not a single canonical modular scale. Each token is a deliberate decision per breakpoint, not ratio-derived. Token-swap-by-breakpoint, not clamp(). See learnings.md G9 (scale audit needs a 'deliberate-token-set' classifier rather than only ad-hoc / matched-scale).

fallbackFontDecision.primary: Adobe Clean Display + Adobe Clean (license required, OTF in assets/fonts/)
fallbackFontDecision.fallback: Source Sans 3 (preferred — Adobe open source, weight 900 ships) → Inter weight 900 with -0.045em letter-spacing → system-ui
fallbackFontDecision.rationale: See DESIGN.md § Font availability note. Source Sans 3 shares Adobe Clean's humanist proportions. Inter 900 is denser at display sizes than Helvetica-family fallbacks.

spacing._source: DESIGN.md spacing + DESIGN.json extensions.spacing; designs/hub/styles/global/grid.css gutter:8px max:1920px; bizpro-hub.html:99-110 --s2a-spacing scale

borderRadius._rules: 16px is THE radius across cards / mega-nav-card / mosaic-tile / product-card. 24px is reserved for app-carousel cards. 32px is reserved for full-bleed section bottom corners (so the next layer can peek through). 75px and 999px are pill CTAs only — never rectangle buttons.

backdrops.name: nav-pill-glass
backdrops.uses: scrolled gnav pill — frosted glass replaces shadow

patterns.name: hero-mosaic-converge
patterns.evidence: 5-col × 3-row image mosaic, scroll-pinned 220–240vh, converges from spread positions to tight 8px-gap grid

patterns.name: scroll-scrubbed-text-reveal
patterns.evidence: [data-ta] line splits + progressive y-offset reveals between 90vh and 40vh

patterns.name: mega-nav-clip-morph
patterns.evidence: panel clip-path expands from gnav-pill geometry to viewport bottom (0.48s, cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1))

patterns.name: morphing-sticky-cta
patterns.evidence: 279×56 pill expands into full pricing modal via GSAP-driven width/height/content morph

patterns.name: editorial-bento-2up
patterns.evidence: two-card row tucked behind hero's 32px rounded bottom (margin-top: -32px)

patterns.name: card-grid-3up
patterns.evidence: mega-nav product grid, perks-row, hub-router carousel

patterns.name: section-bottom-rounded
patterns.evidence: 32px bottom corners on full-bleed sections to reveal next layer

patterns.name: card-dark-flip-hover
patterns.evidence: product-card and use-case card invert to #1a1a1a + white text on hover (200ms ease)

patterns.name: hub-router-accordion
patterns.evidence: 4-card flex row where active card grows from 291px to ~491px and crossfades to #000 (600ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1))

trial._source: designs/hub/styles/sticky-cta.css:189-210; reserved per The One Blue Rule

componentStyle.dualCTAPattern: primary-then-secondary-pill (e.g. Try it free + Buy now)

inputs._reason: The reference pages contain no search or form input. DESIGN.md § Inputs/Fields specifies a fallback (75px pill, 1.5px outline, body-medium type) for downstream prototypes when one is introduced.

systemComponents.name: global-nav
systemComponents.exampleBlock:
[logo + nav-links]
[apps-icon + sign-in pill]
systemComponents.treatment: Frosted-glass pill, fixed top, transparent over hero → opaque on scroll. Logo color flips with state (white wordmark over hero, black on scroll, swapped via display:none not opacity blend). Mega-nav opens via a clip-path expansion from this pill's geometry. Per The Glass-Not-Shadow Rule, never apply a box-shadow here.

systemComponents.name: mega-nav-panel
systemComponents.treatment: Off-canvas dropdown panel triggered by gnav links. clip-path morphs from pill geometry (8px inset all sides, 64px revealed) to full viewport (0 inset, bottom radius 18px) over 0.48s with cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1). Background crossfades transparent → #f8f8f8. Four panes: Products (filter+grid), Use Cases (5-col cards), Solutions (3-col list+feature), Learn & Support (3-col list+stack). Cards stagger in by nth-child at 25ms apart.

systemComponents.name: mobile-nav-overlay
systemComponents.treatment: Hamburger-toggled overlay. Sits behind gnav at z:99 and clip-path-expands from the nav-pill shape — same morph metaphor as the desktop mega-nav. Logo slides off-screen during open.

systemComponents.name: site-footer
systemComponents.treatment: background: #000, white type. 5-column link grid on desktop (6 at widest), stacks mobile-side. Featured-products strip with 16px icons. Legal + social row at bottom (adchoices, CCPA marks). Optional large wordmark with clip-path-reveal on scroll (60% inset → 0%).

systemComponents.name: sticky-cta-pill
systemComponents.treatment: Floating bottom-center 279×56 black pill with two-layer ambient shadow. GSAP-morphs into a full pricing modal with three plan-cards + scrim (rgba(0,0,0,0.6) + 12px blur). Single shape across pill and modal states. The plan-trial pill inside the modal is the only other place trial-blue (#3b63fb) appears.

voice.heroHeadline: With great power comes great productivity.
voice.heroSubcopy: Do your best work faster with AI-powered document insights, secure collaboration, and professional content creation — all in one place.
voice.heroSubcopyAlt: Streamline the way you work, communicate, and transact business. Plus, get AI-powered assistance, APIs and integrations, and built-in security tools.
voice.firstParagraph: Do your best work faster with AI-powered document insights, secure collaboration, and professional content creation — all in one place.

tone.evidence: Single-clause headlines, periods inside section titles ('Real teams. Real work. Real impact.'), em-dashes preferred over commas, sentence case throughout, zero exclamation marks, zero text-transform: uppercase. Confident generosity over capability density.

voiceSamples.text: With great power comes great productivity.
Do your best work faster with AI-powered document insights, secure collaboration, and professional content creation — all in one place.
Work faster, smarter, and more securely with Adobe.
Streamline the way you work, communicate, and transact business. Plus, get AI-powered assistance, APIs and integrations, and built-in security tools.
Work faster. No matter the work.
There's more to Acrobat than Acrobat.
Work smarter with 70+ professional PDF tools to create, edit, sign, share, and more — all powered by AI. And that's just the beginning.
Real teams. Real work. Real impact.
From small businesses to the world's largest enterprises, our customers use Acrobat to unleash their creativity, accelerate document productivity, and power their digital businesses.
Do it all with the power of Acrobat.
Bring together AI-powered insights, intuitive content creation, and secure, real-time collaboration — so teams can easily uncover key findings, create polished reports, and work together quickly.
Turn insights into action and accelerate your pipeline with AI-powered tools.
Take the pain out of campaigns.
Ask your custom or prebuilt AI Assistant to quickly summarize, analyze, provide insights, turn docs into decks, and more.
Share a secure PDF Space with teammates, where everyone can ask AI Assistant to help with research, strategy, planning, or quick learning.
The highly secure PDF and e-signature solution with advanced tools, PDF Spaces, Acrobat AI Assistant, Adobe Express Premium plus admin features.
Get answers, find assets, access your work and create.
Craft at the highest level of creative.
Create quickly and easily with AI models.
Unify content, data and work with AI.
Learn, create, and save on the top tools.

headingFrequency._reason: Source-side counts headings across 5 crawled pages. The target is 2 design references (hub + bizpro-hub) — section-headline corpus is captured in voiceSamples instead, which is a more useful slice for redesign authoring.

toneMetrics._note: Counted across the two reference HTMLs. Zero uppercase headings — Acrobat Studio uses sentence case throughout, in deliberate contrast to the source-side Semrush register (27% uppercase headings).

crossPromo._reason: Source-side cross-promo detection clusters headings repeated on 5+ pages (e.g. 'Start Here', 'GET STARTED WITH SEMRUSH TODAY'). The target is 2 design references — there is no cross-page repetition to detect. The closest parallel — Acrobat's reusable section-headline pattern — is the eyebrow + Title 2 + body-md trio captured in motifs.patterns and voice.tone.
